title: The Most Expensive Home on Long Island’s North Fork Just Listed for $16.8 Million
summary: The 4.25-acre compound on Cutchogue's Nassau Point has more than 300 feet of sandy beachfront and a deep-water dock.

fullArticle:
A waterfront estate on Long Island’s North Fork is poised to set a new price record for the regional real estate market. Located on the prestigious Nassau Point peninsula in Cutchogue, the 4.25-acre spread pairs more than 300 feet of shoreline along Peconic Bay with a shingle-clad residential compound positioned to maximize sunset views over the water.

The main house, tucked behind artist-designed iron gates at the end of a winding driveway, was completed in 2006. Interiors are defined by detailed millwork, coffered ceilings, and expansive windows. In addition to spacious living and dining rooms, there are a semicircular media lounge, a family room, and a 35-foot-long eat-in kitchen with two ranges, two sinks, and two refrigerators.
